methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Mitra Chem is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Vivas Kumar and Will Fitzgerald that has raised $60M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 9 peer companies—1 public and 8 private—all operating in the Climate & Energy sector at or near Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $60M total capital raised as a valuation proxy, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $114M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage comparable companies: Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), producing a median valuation of $355M with a range from $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects material differences in technology maturity and capital intensity across the climate tech landscape.

The triangulated valuation range is $97M–$408M, with a median of $234M across both methods. The stage-progression method yields a more conservative estimate anchored to deployment of capital, while peer-median reflects the higher valuations observed in the sector. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Mitra Chem's technology differentiation and market position align with high-end comparables like Commonwealth Fusion Systems versus mid-range peers, and sensitivity to the volatile maturity and capital requirements characterizing climate technology investors' expectations at Series B.
